citation.anchor: invalidate any ALAT entry at any

preconditions: Physical WBL memory holds one literal eight-byte GR20 payload and one literal single-precision FR21 payload
LD8.A r20 and LDFS.A f21 retire before the debugger checkpoint
No CHK.A executes before the source stops at bundle 0x40
inputs: One source QEMU with a GDB software breakpoint at 0x40
One HMP savevm checkpoint and a distinct destination QEMU that loads it
Same-type GR20/FR21 and cross-type FR20/GR21 CHK.A probes
expected_results: Source and destination guest-visible checkpoints are exact apart from nonarchitectural current-slot diagnostics
Direct and migrated same-type checks each produce a valid hit-or-pessimistic-miss marker
Both direct and migrated cross-type checks take mandatory recovery
Both continuations reach terminal IP 0x100 without interruption
allowed_results: GR20 and FR21 may independently remain present or be pessimistically invalidated before either same-type check
The direct and migrated optional check outcomes need not equal each other
Internal replacement and transient lookup state is not a guest-visible oracle
cases.committed_prefix: The two advanced loads commit before the external debugger stop; the restore may retain or discard entries but cannot manufacture or retag one
cases.restart_action: A fresh destination resumes directly at bundle 0x40 and completes the same optional-hit and mandatory-cross-type safety graph

result_observations: source and destination direct typed traces

oracle.derivation: Addresses, payloads, GR/FR target identities, checkpoint IP, recovery graph, legal optional outcomes, mandatory cross-type misses, and checkpoint comparison are test-owned literals derived from Intel Volume 1 sections 4.4.5.3.1 and 4.4.5.3.2; no production ALAT or migration helper supplies an expected guest result

limitations: Reset and replay treatment remain separate ALT-007 atomic rows
The test exercises physical WBL entries; translated-address persistence remains covered at the serializer field level rather than this runtime path

citation.anchor: A new entry is allocated in the ALAT which contains the new ALAT register tag, the load access size, and a tag derived from the physical memory address

preconditions: The current ALAT wire version is one and contains thirty-two entry payloads plus one replacement cursor
The optional target-type subsection distinguishes GR and FR entries while an absent subsection defaults to legacy GR
Transient valid masks, GR masks, and reference counts are explicitly outside the wire image
inputs: Eight active legal entries crossing all GR and FR widths, low/high targets, physical/virtual address modes, distinct 64-bit addresses, and cursor 31
Every lower/upper invalid GR and FR target partition
Every gap and outer invalid width partition for GR and FR
Invalid cursors 32 and 255 plus versions -1, 0, and 2
One inactive entry with arbitrary ignored target, width, physical, and address payload
expected_results: Every persistent field of all eight active entries and cursor 31 round-trips exactly
All transient masks and refcounts load clear
Every malformed active field, schema target type, cursor, and unsupported version is rejected
The same malformed field rows fail both incoming post-load and outgoing pre-save paths
The inactive arbitrary payload remains accepted because only its target-type schema metadata is live
allowed_results: Inactive non-type payload is preserved or ignored without becoming a live entry
The generic VMState layer may report additional transport framing errors before the ALAT callback
Error text is diagnostic and is not the result oracle
permitted_state_changes: The isolated destination ALAT persistent image on successful load
The isolated transient summaries, which must become zero
Host error state for a rejected save or load
required_unchanged_state: No CPU or guest memory exists in the isolated validator fixture
Rejected candidates cannot become an accepted destination image
expected_faults: EINVAL from the ALAT validator for each malformed semantic field or unsupported version
cases.committed_prefix: Successful v1 input publishes every persistent field atomically; any rejected semantic field prevents an accepted image and any rejected output prevents a valid checkpoint
cases.restart_action: No guest restart exists; the host save/load call returns success only for the complete valid image and EINVAL for each malformed candidate

result_observations: outgoing pre-save and VMState save return

oracle.derivation: The legal GR/FR target ranges, complete width sets, thirty-two-entry cursor boundary, field literals, invalid equivalence partitions, and version outcomes are declared in the test from the cited ALAT definition and frozen v1 wire contract; they are not enumerated from the production validator under test

limitations: Generic truncated-stream and I/O transport failures are owned by QEMU's common VMState tests
This atomic row validates the current v1 semantic field schema rather than compatibility with a future version
